title: rhinanthus alectorolophus subsp. facchinii
intro: Rhinanthus alectorolophus subsp. facchinii, also known as the "yellow rattle", is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae, which is a type of parasitic plant that derives its nutrients from the roots of surrounding plants. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Rhinanthus alectorolophus subsp. facchinii include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are commonly consumed to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
